title: arceuthobium campylopodum subsp. occidentale
intro: Arceuthobium campylopodum subsp. occidentale, also known as the western dwarf mistletoe, is a parasitic plant species native to the western regions of North America. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat respiratory issues and its anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been traditionally used to relieve symptoms of colds, coughs, and bronchitis, as well as to soothe sore throats and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of western dwarf mistletoe include teas and infusions, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water, or by using the plant in combination with other herbs to create a medicinal decoction.
